name: agent
tagline: Chat with your Claude Code sessions — a session per agent.

events.title: A message arrived for an agent

actions.title: Deliver an inbound message (wakes the session)

provision.note: hub registers a vault runtime trigger that webhooks the agent inbound endpoint with an agent:send bearer

actions.title: Reload a changed agent definition (re-instantiate the agent)

provision.note: hub registers a vault runtime trigger that webhooks the agent-def reload endpoint with an agent:send bearer; the daemon re-reads the one changed #agent/definition note and (re)instantiates that agent

connectionTemplates.title: Link an agent to a vault
connectionTemplates.description: Back an agent with a Parachute vault: inbound notes wake the agent, replies are written back as notes. The operator picks WHICH vault (the parameter) and names the agent's channel; the agent's config UI creates the connection with the operator's approval (the click is the approval).

parameters.title: Vault
parameters.description: Which vault stores this channel's messages — the operator chooses from the hub's installed vaults.

parameters.title: Channel name
parameters.description: A unique slug naming the channel the session connects to.

connectionTemplates.title: Reload agent definitions on create
connectionTemplates.description: Make a new #agent/definition note instantiate its agent live, instead of converging only via the 60s loadAll poll. The operator picks WHICH def-vault (the parameter); the connection's creation in the hub Connections view is the operator's approval. Pair with reload-defs-on-edit to cover edits too — the hub binds one event per connection, so create and edit are two connections.

parameters.title: Def-vault
parameters.description: Which vault holds the #agent/definition notes — the operator chooses from the hub's installed vaults.

connectionTemplates.title: Reload agent definitions on edit
connectionTemplates.description: Make an edited #agent/definition note re-instantiate its agent live, instead of converging only via the 60s loadAll poll. The operator picks WHICH def-vault (the parameter); the connection's creation in the hub Connections view is the operator's approval. Pair with reload-defs-on-create to cover creates too — the hub binds one event per connection, so create and edit are two connections.

parameters.title: Def-vault
parameters.description: Which vault holds the #agent/definition notes — the operator chooses from the hub's installed vaults.
